You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its score and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Artist. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)

"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to format a list with commas and "and"
    def fmt_list(items: list[str]) -> str:
        if not items:
            return ""
        if len(items) == 1:
            return items[0]
        return ", ".join(items[:-1]) + " and " + items[-1]

    # Index triples by subject preserving order of first appearance
    from collections import defaultdict
    subj_map: dict[str, list[Triple]] = defaultdict(list)
    order: list[str] = []
    for t in triples:
        subj_map[t.subject].append(t)
        if t.subject not in order:
            order.append(t.subject)

    sentences: list[str] = []

    for subj in order:
        parts: list[str] = []
        triples_here = subj_map[subj]

        # ----- genre -----
        genre_objs = [t.object for t in triples_here if t.predicate == "genre"]
        if genre_objs:
            genre_phrase = f"{subj} is a {genre_objs[0].lower()} artist"
            # stylistic origin of the genre (genre as subject)
            origins = [t.object for t in triples if t.subject == genre_objs[0] and t.predicate == "stylisticOrigin"]
            if origins:
                genre_phrase += f", originating from {origins[0].lower()}"
            parts.append(genre_phrase)

        # ----- associated band / musical artist (multiple) -----
        assoc_objs = [t.object for t in triples_here if t.predicate == "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist"]
        if assoc_objs:
            parts.append(f"{subj} is associated with {fmt_list(assoc_objs)}")

        # ----- instrument (multiple) -----
        instr_objs = [t.object for t in triples_here if t.predicate == "instrument"]
        if instr_objs:
            # If the subject looks like a genre (contains space or is capitalised) treat instrument as used in genre
            if any(word[0].isupper() for word in subj.split()):
                parts.append(f"The {fmt_list(instr_objs).lower()} is an instrument used in {subj}")
            else:
                parts.append(f"{subj} plays the {fmt_list(instr_objs)}")

        # ----- occupation (single) -----
        occ_objs = [t.object for t in triples_here if t.predicate == "occupation"]
        if occ_objs:
            occ = occ_objs[0].lower()
            # add article if not already present
            if not occ.startswith(("a ", "an ", "the ")):
                occ = "a " + occ
            parts.append(f"{subj} is {occ}")

        # ----- birth / death / origin info -----
        for t in triples_here:
            p, o = t.predicate, t.object
            if p in ("birthDate", "birthYear"):
                parts.append(f"{subj} was born in {o}")
            elif p in ("deathDate", "deathPlace"):
                parts.append(f"{subj} died in {o}")
            elif p == "birthPlace":
                parts.append(f"{subj} was born in {o}")
            elif p == "origin":
                parts.append(f"{subj} originates from {o}")
            elif p == "nationality":
                parts.append(f"{subj} is {o}")
            elif p == "recordLabel":
                parts.append(f"{subj} is signed to {o}")
            elif p == "background":
                parts.append(f"{subj} has a background as {o}")
            elif p == "activeYearsStartYear":
                parts.append(f"{subj} started activity in {o}")
            elif p == "activeYearsEndYear":
                parts.append(f"{subj} ended activity in {o}")

        # ----- relational predicates where object is primary -----
        relational_predicates = {
            "leader", "capital", "currency", "anthem", "parentCompany", "location",
            "governingBody", "leaderTitle", "country", "isPartOf", "derivative",
            "bandMember"
        }
        for t in triples_here:
            if t.predicate in relational_predicates:
                parts.append(f"{t.object} is the {t.predicate} of {subj}")

        # Combine parts for this subject into a single sentence
        if parts:
            sentence = fmt_list(parts)
            # Capitalise first character
            sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:] + "."
            sentences.append(sentence)

    # Join all subject‑level sentences with a space
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
